title: Punjabi Kadhi Pakora

ingredients.name: sour curd (well-beaten)
visual_checks: sour curd gives the best flavour; use 1-day-old curd
ingredients.notes: sour curd gives the best flavour; use 1-day-old curd

subs.name: whisked coconut yogurt (unsweetened) for a dairy-free version; the tang may be less pronounced

ingredients.name: besan (chickpea flour)
visual_checks: 3 tbsp for kadhi, 2 tbsp for pakoras
ingredients.notes: 3 tbsp for kadhi, 2 tbsp for pakoras

subs.name: no substitute for besan in kadhi — it is the defining thickener

ingredients.name: onion, thinly sliced
visual_checks: half for pakoras, half for tempering
ingredients.notes: half for pakoras, half for tempering

subs.name: half a tsp of red chilli flakes scattered over the final tempering oil
skip if unavailable; add an extra pinch of asafoetida instead

steps.text: Make the kadhi base: whisk the curd with 3 tbsp besan, 0.75 tsp turmeric, red chilli powder, salt and 3 cups water until completely smooth with no lumps. Strain through a sieve if needed.

Make the pakora batter: mix 2 tbsp besan with half the sliced onion, 1 green chilli, grated ginger, a pinch of turmeric and salt. Add water a little at a time to make a thick batter that coats the onion — about 3–4 tbsp water.

Heat oil for deep-frying in a kadai to about 170°C (a small drop of batter should rise within 3 seconds). Drop heaped spoonfuls of the pakora batter into the oil and fry in batches for 3–4 minutes until crisp and golden. Drain on kitchen paper.

Heat 2 tbsp oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium flame. Add fenugreek seeds and cumin; let them crackle. Add the remaining sliced onion and fry until lightly golden. Pour in the curd-besan liquid, stirring continuously to prevent lumps.

Bring the kadhi to a boil while stirring, then reduce to a gentle simmer. Cook uncovered for 20 minutes, stirring every few minutes, until the kadhi thickens and any raw besan flavour has cooked out. Add the pakoras and simmer 5 more minutes so they soak up the gravy.

Make the final tempering: heat 2 tbsp oil in a small pan until smoking hot. Add the dried red chillies and asafoetida; let them darken for 15 seconds. Pour this sizzling tempering over the kadhi and do not stir — it looks most dramatic on the table. Serve with steamed rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → whisked coconut yogurt (unsweetened) for a dairy-free version; the tang may be less pronounced; besan → no substitute for besan in kadhi — it is the defining thickener; fenugreek-seeds → skip if unavailable; add an extra pinch of asafoetida instead; dried-red-chilli → half a tsp of red chilli flakes scattered over the final tempering oil
